subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Boards
- **5 farmyards** — One per player color.
- **3 game boards** — Central action boards.
- **1 major improvements board** — Includes scoring summary on reverse.

#### Cards (360 Total)
- **169 Occupation cards** — Yellow cards.
- **139 Minor Improvement cards** — Orange cards, including 7 upgrade cards.
- **10 Major Improvement cards** — Red cards.
- **14 Round cards** — Blue cards for rounds 1 to 14.
- **16 Action cards** — Green, player-count dependent.
- **5 Begging cards** — Grey cards.
- **5 Summary cards** — Player aids.
- **2 Deck cards** — Deck I and Deck K.

#### Occupation Card Availability by Player Count
--- 1–5 Players ---
- **66 Occupation cards** — Cards marked for 1–5 players.

--- 3–5 Players ---
- **41 Occupation cards** — Additional cards marked for 3–5 players.

--- 4–5 Players ---
- **62 Occupation cards** — Additional cards marked for 4–5 players.

#### Wooden Player Pieces (Per Color, 5 Colors Total)
- **5 family member discs** — Worker pieces per player color.
- **4 stables** — Per player color.
- **15 fences** — Per player color.

#### Resources & Animals
- **33 wood counters** — Dark brown.
- **27 clay counters** — Light brown.
- **15 reed counters** — White.
- **18 stone counters** — Grey.
- **27 grain counters** — Yellow.
- **18 vegetable counters** — Orange.
- **21 sheep cubes** — White animal tokens.
- **18 wild boar cubes** — Black animal tokens.
- **15 cattle cubes** — Brown animal tokens.
- **1 starting player token** — First-player marker.

#### Tiles & Markers
- **33 field/stone-house tiles** — Brown/grey.
- **24 wood/clay-hut tiles** — Brown/red.
- **36 food markers** — Value 1 each.
- **9 multiplication markers** — Multipliers for goods/animals/food.
- **3 claim markers** — “Guest” on reverse.
- **1 scoring pad** — Final score sheet.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Central Setup Sequence
1. **Place boards** — Put the 3 game boards in the center.
2. **Place major improvements** — Put all 10 red major improvements face up on the major improvements board.
3. **Sort supply** — Place resources, animals, food, fences, stables, and house/hut tiles in supply piles.
4. **Build round deck** — Sort blue round cards by stage, shuffle each stage, then stack with Stage 1 on top and Stage 6 on bottom.
5. **Place begging cards** — Put 5 grey begging cards face up near the boards.
6. **Give summary cards** — Each player takes 1 summary card.

#### Green Action Cards by Player Count
--- 1 Player ---
- **Green action cards used** — 0 cards.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Green action cards used** — 0 cards.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Green action cards used** — 4 cards face up.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Green action cards used** — 6 cards face up.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Green action cards used** — 6 cards face up.

#### Family Game Variant Board Notes
- **Reverse side use** — Game board 1 reverse is used for Family Game.
- **Rule impact** — Family Game changes some action spaces and removes Occupation/Minor Improvement play.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Personal Board Setup
- **Choose a color** — Take all pieces in one player color.
- **Take farmyard** — Take 1 farmyard board.
- **Place rooms** — Put 2 wooden hut room tiles on starting house spaces.
- **Place family members** — Put 1 family member in each starting room (2 active workers at start).
- **Reserve pieces** — Keep extra family members, fences, and stables in personal reserve.

#### Card Setup (Full Game)
- **Filter Occupations** — Remove Occupation cards not valid for current player count.
- **Deal Occupations** — Deal 7 Occupation cards to each player.
- **Deal Minor Improvements** — Deal 7 Minor Improvement cards to each player.
- **Set aside leftovers** — Unused cards remain out of play.

#### Family Game Variant
- **Occupation cards** — Not used.
- **Minor Improvement cards** — Not used.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **1–5 players supported** — Base game range listed in rulebook text.
- **3-player scaling** — Use 4 green action cards.
- **4-player scaling** — Use 6 green action cards.
- **5-player scaling** — Use 6 green action cards.
- **Solo/2-player scaling** — Use 0 green action cards.
- **Occupation deck scaling** — 66 cards for 1–5, +41 cards for 3–5, +62 cards for 4–5.
- **Family Game variant** — Removes Occupation/Minor Improvement cards and changes some action spaces.

#### Starting Resources
- **Starting player** — Chosen player gets Starting Player marker and 2 food.
- **Other players** — Every other player gets 3 food.

#### First-Player Rule
- **No automatic pass** — Starting Player marker does not move automatically each round.
- **Marker movement** — Marker moves only when a player takes the Start Player action.

#### Start Round 1
- **Begin play** — Start with round 1 and the normal four-phase round flow.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Game Length
- **14 rounds** — Total rounds in the game.
- **6 stages** — Rounds are grouped into six stages.
- **Harvest rounds** — Harvest occurs after rounds 4, 7, 9, 11, 13, and 14.

#### Round Sequence
1. **Start Round** — Reveal top round card and place it on the matching round space.
2. **Apply new action** — Newly revealed action is available now and in all later rounds.
3. **Resolve beginning effects** — Resolve any start-of-round effects from cards.
4. **Replenish** — Add goods/food/animals to every accumulation space with arrow icons.
5. **Work Phase** — In clockwise order from starting player, each player places 1 family member at a time and resolves action immediately.
6. **Continue placements** — Repeat until all players have placed all available family members.
7. **Return Home** — Remove all family members from action spaces to home rooms.
8. **Harvest check** — If this round ends a stage, perform Harvest.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Core Placement Rules
- **One space, one person** — Each action space may be used by only one family member per round unless a rule says otherwise.
- **One worker at a time** — Players place exactly one family member each turn during Work Phase.
- **No idle placement** — A family member cannot occupy a space without performing its action.

#### Action Families
- **Resource gathering** — Take wood, clay, reed, stone, grain, vegetables, and food from legal spaces.
- **Farm development** — Plow fields, sow crops, build rooms, build stables, build fences, create pastures.
- **Livestock actions** — Take animals and place them immediately in legal housing.
- **Tempo actions** — Take Start Player, Family Growth, Renovation, and other timing-sensitive actions.
- **Card actions** — Play Occupations, Minor Improvements, and buy Major Improvements when action spaces allow.

#### Multi-Option Space Handling
- **Either/or spaces** — Choose one listed branch if text gives alternatives.
- **Ordered spaces** — If text says one action is taken after another, follow that order exactly.
- **No partial inversion** — Do not skip required first steps to take optional later steps.

#### Resource Visibility Rules
- **Open supply info** — Resources in personal supply must stay visible to other players.
- **Card visibility** — Played cards may not be fully covered.
- **Read aloud rule** — When playing a card or buying a major improvement, read its text aloud.

#### Animal Handling Rules
- **No personal animal stash** — Animals cannot be stored in personal supply.
- **Immediate placement** — Place animals directly into legal farmyard housing.
- **Illegal overflow** — If animals cannot be housed, return them to supply unless immediately converted via a valid improvement ability.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Harvest System
- **Timing** — Harvest happens at end of each stage.
- **Feeding pressure** — During Feeding, if you cannot or choose not to feed fully, take 1 Begging card per missing food.
- **Penalty source** — Begging cards are negative points at endgame.

#### Family Growth Tension
- **Action gain** — More family members means more placements each round.
- **Food burden** — More family members also increases harvest feeding requirements.

#### Round-Card Tempo
- **Action growth** — Each revealed round card permanently expands the action set.
- **Window timing** — Delayed actions can be blocked by turn order pressure as options expand.

#### Player-Count Scaling
--- 1 Player ---
- **Green action cards** — None used.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Green action cards** — None used.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Green action cards** — 4 used.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Green action cards** — 6 used.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Green action cards** — 6 used.

#### Occupation Deck Scaling
--- 1–5 Players ---
- **Base Occupations** — 66 cards are valid.

--- 3–5 Players ---
- **Additional Occupations** — 41 more cards become valid.

--- 4–5 Players ---
- **Additional Occupations** — 62 more cards become valid.

#### Family Game Variant
- **Card removal** — Occupation and Minor Improvement cards are removed.
- **Action-space change** — Some action spaces differ on Family Game board side.
- **Core flow kept** — Round/harvest/placement structure remains in place.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Final round** — Game ends after round 14 and final harvest.

#### Scoring Categories
- **Fields** — Score by field count band.
- **Pastures** — Score by pasture count band.
- **Grain** — Score planted/harvested grain count band.
- **Vegetables** — Score planted/harvested vegetable count band.
- **Sheep** — Score by sheep count band.
- **Wild boar** — Score by wild boar count band.
- **Cattle** — Score by cattle count band.

#### Fixed Bonuses & Penalties
- **Unused spaces** — -1 point per unused farmyard space.
- **Fenced stable** — +1 point per fenced stable.
- **Clay room** — +1 point per clay hut room.
- **Stone room** — +2 points per stone house room.
- **Family members** — +3 points per family member.
- **Card points** — Add card-based points from Occupations and Improvements.
- **Begging penalties** — Subtract penalties from all Begging cards taken.

#### Winner
- **Highest total** — Player with highest final score wins.
- **Tie handling** — Use official tie rule for your edition/scenario.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First-Stage Priorities
- **Feed first** — Build a reliable feeding plan before first harvest pressure.
- **Round 3–4 target** — Have a concrete food engine online by rounds 3–4.
- **Growth discipline** — Take Family Growth only when food support is already credible.
- **Board cleanup early** — Convert empty farm spaces into productive fields/pastures/rooms early to avoid -1 penalties later.

#### Tempo Planning
- **Round reveal prep** — Track upcoming round-card unlocks and pre-collect costs for key actions.
- **Start player timing** — Take Start Player when contested action access next round is critical.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Early overgrowth** — Growing family too early without food support leads to Begging card collapse.
- **Illegal animal intake** — Taking animals without legal housing wastes actions and resources.
- **Late-board neglect** — Ignoring empty farm penalties until final scoring gives away many points.
- **Single-axis scoring** — Overcommitting one category while taking negatives elsewhere usually loses to balanced farms.
- **Action text shortcuts** — Misreading ordered spaces (especially “after” clauses) creates illegal sequencing.
- **Resource hoarding** — Holding stockpiles without timely conversion misses compounding value from earlier builds.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Family Growth Timing
- **Early growth upside** — More actions across more rounds.
- **Early growth risk** — Larger harvest feeding burden sooner.

#### Decision 2: Renovation vs Farm Spread
- **Renovation line** — Push house upgrades and related card synergies.
- **Farm spread line** — Push fields/pastures/animals to avoid category gaps.

#### Decision 3: Card Engine vs Board Tempo
- **Card-engine line** — Invest in Occupation/Improvement synergies early.
- **Board-tempo line** — Prioritize baseline farm infrastructure before deep combo setup.

#### Decision 4: Start Player vs Immediate Yield
- **Marker control** — Secure first pick next round for contested spaces.
- **Immediate yield** — Take large accumulation spaces now if timing pressure is low.

metadata.notes: Reformatted to skimmable style with explicit player-count audit and exact count headers where rulebook text provides values.
